title: Share of people worried about terrorism
subtitle: Respondents who said they worried "very much" or "a great deal".
caption:
Share of people worried about terrorism

Respondents who said they worried "very much" or "a great deal".

This choropleth world map displays the share of people worried about terrorism, based on respondents who said they worried "very much" or "a great deal". The legend spans from 0% to 100%, with specific tick points at 20%, 40%, 60%, 80%, and 100%. The darkest red shades, indicating the highest levels of concern, are visible in countries such as Mexico, the Philippines, and parts of the Middle East including Iraq and Iran. These nations fall into the upper bands of the legend, suggesting values above 60%. In contrast, many European countries, including Germany, France, and the United Kingdom, appear in lighter shades, indicating lower levels of worry, generally below 40%. North America shows a mix, with the United States in a mid-range shade and Canada in a lighter shade. South America displays a range of colors, with some countries like Brazil and Argentina in mid-range shades, while others like Chile and Uruguay are in lighter shades. Africa has several countries in the lightest shades, indicating very low levels of worry, while a few, such as Nigeria, are in darker shades. Australia and New Zealand are in mid-range shades. Many countries, particularly in central Africa and parts of Asia, are marked with diagonal lines, indicating no data available for these regions. The visual pattern shows higher levels of worry concentrated in certain regions of the Americas, the Middle East, and Southeast Asia, with lower levels in much of Europe and parts of Africa.
